title: 1.1: Tests that marking multiple prompts with OSC 133;A escape sequence allows backward navigation through prompt history to reach progressively older prompts until reaching the oldest available prompt.
1.2: Tests that navigating forward through prompts returns boundary indication (False or failure) when attempting to navigate beyond the most recent prompt
1.3:  Tests that jumping to last visited prompt position retrieves command output from the previously visited location after navigating away to different scroll positions.
1.4: Tests that capturing first command output visible on screen produces different result than capturing most recent command output when multiple command outputs exist on screen
1.5: Tests that extracting command output with ANSI escape sequences preserved includes the escape codes in captured output when preservation is explicitly enabled and excluding them when disabled
1.6: Tests that retrieving command output after terminal has been resized with reflowed prompts successfully returns complete multi-line output content without losing or corrupting data
1.7: Tests that retrieving command output extending beyond visible screen into scrollback buffer (pager history) successfully captures output lines including those in scrollback
1.8: Tests that handling last non-empty command output when recent commands produce no output returns the most recent command output that contains content, skipping empty outputs
1.9: Tests that managing multi-line command output capture with text wrapping across screen boundaries due to exceeding column width successfully captures all content including wrapped portions

1.10: Tests that tracking output positions correctly when scrolled up in history versus at current prompt shows that output retrieval location awareness changes between scrolled-up state and bottom state
1.11: Tests that capturing command output after downward scrolling operations (scrolling from older prompts toward newer prompts) successfully retrieves output after downward scrolling operations.
2.1: Places the test for prompt marking in kitty_tests/screen.py.
3.1: All tests use self.ae() assertion method inherited from BaseTest class for equality assertions.
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern test_<functionality> using descriptive names indicating the test document metadata behavior
4.1: Follows the pattern of using screen.draw() method followed by screen.carriage_return() to simulate text drawing for testcase checking content generation
4.2: Follows the pattern of using self.create_screen() to create test screen instances for testcase checking screen manipulation.
